INT. SCHOOL CLASSROOM - GREEN TOWN - DAY

A big, almost empty schoolroom in a small American town in a timeless age between the late l920's and the early l930's. The SILENCE is underlined by the breathy TICK of a schoolroom clock and the SCRATCHING of two pens. We SEE TWO YOUNG BOYS sitting at their desks writing. Their names are WILL RALLOWAY and JIM NIGHTSHADE. Will is as fair as Jim is dark. The only other person in the room is MISS FOLEY, the schoolmistress. She is about forty, very plain indeed, with a dress buttoning up to her neck and an expression of sadness. On the blackboard behind her, in copperplate handwriting, is the line that the boys have to write out as a punishment: "We must not whisper to each other in class." ANOTHER ANGLE We SEE Will, his paper covered with the lines, writing out the last of them. Then we SEE Jim. He is drawing on an odd sheet of paper, a cruel caricature of Miss Foley, her beaky nose, her jutting chin. As Jim finishes drawing, he HEARS a step behind him and, opening the lid of his desk a crack, slides the drawing in. ANOTHER ANGLE Miss Foley has left her desk and is standing behind the boys. TITLES END
Finished?
Yes, Miss Foley.

you. I will not have whispering.

(as he goes) s-et dreams, Miss Foley!

we SEE Will and Jim, one light, one dark, both good friends, each smart enough not to beat the other, running, leaping fire hydrants, vaulting iron railings, THUNDERING on pavement lids. It seems to lift the boys as they run, arms out, leaping and turning like young ponies galloping free in the swirling air.
(jumping) Jump higher'n you! Never!

(runs) Not older! We were born the same night.
